<!-- topic: release -->

# Release

[edikt:directives:start]: #
- A single SHA256SUMS file MUST be shipped as a GitHub Release asset containing one line per tarball in the standard sha256sum format. (ref: ADR-013)
- Signing (GPG / Sigstore / cosign) MUST be deferred to a future ADR and MUST NOT be bundled with the checksum format release. (ref: ADR-013)
- `bin/edikt install` MUST fetch `SHA256SUMS` alongside the payload tarball and verify the launcher tarball's hash before extracting. (ref: ADR-013)
- If cosign is not installed, the installer MAY abort with an actionable install instruction unless EDIKT_INSTALL_INSECURE=1 is set. (ref: ADR-016)
- MUST NOT publish separate .pem or .sig files alongside the signature — the SHA256SUMS.sig.bundle is self-contained. (ref: ADR-016)
- MUST NOT use main or releases/latest/download/ as user-facing install surfaces. (ref: ADR-016)
- The README install command and all documentation MUST pin to a specific release tag (releases/download/v<PINNED>/install.sh). (ref: ADR-016)
- The launcher MUST download SHA256SUMS and SHA256SUMS.sig.bundle. (ref: ADR-016)
- The launcher MUST download the edikt-payload-<tag>.tar.gz Release asset (NOT the auto-generated archive). (ref: ADR-016)
- The launcher MUST grep SHA256SUMS for the payload filename and assert the SHA matches the downloaded bytes. (ref: ADR-016)
- The launcher MUST verify the bundle signature using cosign with a regex-form identity assertion. (ref: ADR-016)
- The release workflow MUST publish SHA256SUMS alongside the payload as a Release asset, covering every asset in the release. (ref: ADR-016)
- The release workflow MUST publish exactly one canonical payload artifact per release tag: edikt-payload-<tag>.tar.gz as a GitHub Release asset (NOT the auto-generated archive). (ref: ADR-016)
- The release workflow MUST sign SHA256SUMS with Sigstore keyless: requesting a short-lived signing certificate from Fulcio via GitHub OIDC token, signing SHA256SUMS, and publishing the combined signature + certificate as a single SHA256SUMS.sig.bundle Release asset. (ref: ADR-016)
[edikt:directives:end]: #
[edikt:directives:sha256]: # 8cd518bcf1d61545e8b76c91e83c4066f5511448849c40408634e784ef7074e3

[edikt:prohibitions:start]: #
## Prohibitions
- MUST NOT embed checksums inline in the GitHub Release body — not machine-readable and not suitable for automated pipeline consumption. (ref: ADR-013)
- MUST NOT skip signing and ship ADR-013 wiring fixes only — CRIT-7 remains open, leaving a transient repo compromise able to compromise future installs. (ref: ADR-016)
- MUST NOT use GPG signing with maintainer-held keys — requires a private key kept secret forever, key rotation procedures, and key loss means no more signed releases. (ref: ADR-016)
- MUST NOT use per-file .sha256 sidecars (e.g. edikt-v<ver>.tar.gz.sha256) — verify_against_sidecar() would need to know the sidecar filename and release asset count grows linearly with artifact count. (ref: ADR-013)
[edikt:prohibitions:end]: #
[edikt:prohibitions:sha256]: # e45f6ca0e305c04735ae5b82ed91c08377585e231aaad0a640cf75878621ce3f

[edikt:manual:end]: #
